sourceMovie.title: Licence to Kill
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Licence to Kill works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Spectre
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Licence to Kill clicked for you, Spectre (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Quantum of Solace
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Licence to Kill clicked for you, Quantum of Solace (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Licence to Kill?
faq.answer: Try Spectre, Quantum of Solace, The World Is Not Enough—each shares DNA with Licence to Kill while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Licence to Kill?
faq.answer: Licence to Kill (1989) is best described as Adventure, Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Licence to Kill part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "James Bond Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
